mission.title: Install and Configure Tikv on Kubernetes
mission.description: TiKV is an open-source, distributed, and transactional key-value database that provides ACID-compliant transactional APIs. Installing TiKV allows you to leverage its powerful features for managing large-scale data with high consistency and horizontal scalability.

steps.title: Add the TiKV Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the TiKV Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add tikv https://charts.pingcap.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install TiKV using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install TiKV using the Helm chart, specifying the desired namespace.
```bash
helm install tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --create-namespace --version v8.5.5
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the TiKV pods are running successfully in the specified namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace tikv
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the TiKV deployment. Edit the values file or use the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --set resources.limits.cpu=2000m --set resources.limits.memory=4Gi
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring (optional)
steps.description:
For better observability, you can enable monitoring by integrating TiKV with Prometheus. Update your Helm installation:
```bash
helm upgrade tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --set monitoring.enabled=true
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of TiKV will show the TiKV pods running in the 'tikv' namespace. You can also verify the resource limits and monitoring settings applied to the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add tikv https://charts.pingcap.org
helm repo update
helm install tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --create-namespace --version v8.5.5
helm upgrade tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --set resources.limits.cpu=2000m --set resources.limits.memory=4Gi
helm upgrade tikv tikv/tikv --namespace tikv --set monitoring.enabled=true

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine.
